tests.source_program:
define void @test_guard_assume_and(ptr nocapture readonly %data, i64 %count) {
entry:
  %cmp.ult = icmp ult i64 %count, 5
  %cmp.ne = icmp ne i64 %count, 0
  %cmp.and = and i1 %cmp.ult, %cmp.ne
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp.and)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %idx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %data, i64 %iv
  store i32 1, ptr %idx, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond.not = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %count
  br i1 %exitcond.not, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }

issue.title: [SCEV] applyLoopGuards drops information on SCEV builtin_assumes()
issue.body:
applyLoopGuards tries to rewrite a SCEV expression using assumes.
The order of the assumes in the source code affects the rewritten SCEV and sometimes we lose information. 
An example is https://godbolt.org/z/vYnvKcrME where the vectorizer can't prove tripcount % VF = 0 despite of the assumption with that information.
If the order of the assumes is changed as in https://godbolt.org/z/Wf445MMha code is optimized.
This is since the assume (tripcount > 0) rewrites a new SCEV that loses the divisibility information.
Need to think of a way to conserve the already rewritten expression of a certain value.
issue.author: komalon1

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Interesting cases! Review to add IR tests available: https://reviews.llvm.org/D126460
